entry.title: Busting the Top 3 Myths about Cloud ERP Implementation
entry.content:
An Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) implementation can revolutionize all aspects of your business processes. Let’s dive into the top three common myths associated with ERP implementation and discover the truths behind them. 

 

#1 Myth: ERP is for large enterprises.  

When people think about ERP applications, they often assume that only large or multinational enterprises implement these solutions. The truth is quite different than this. 

For years, small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) remained on the sidelines, while their larger competitors enjoyed the advantages of ERP systems.  However, times have changed, and the latest research tells a different story - around 80% of SAP’s customers are small and midsize companies, choosing SAP solutions that help them simplify, automate, and grow their businesses. 

Resistance to change can complexify and prolong the process in both large multinational companies and SMEs. What businesses should understand is that a successful ERP implementation is an ongoing journey of continuous improvement.  

No matter how you turn it, implementing ERP is an extensive project and requires careful planning. Fortunately, SAP tools like Digital Discovery Assessment (DDA) and Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (CALM) assist customers throughout the transition. 

  

#2 Myth: Cloud ERP is less secure than on-premises. 

On-Premise solutions have become a cornerstone in organizations’ technological infrastructure around the world.  Given its long-standing tenure, the thinking often results in believing that on-premises are more secure.  In reality, major cloud data centers have stronger security measures due to dedicated teams solely focused on guarding against security breaches. 

Cloud providers offer a diverse array of security features and services aimed at assisting businesses in safeguarding their data and applications. Utilizing a cloud ERP system allows businesses to enhance their security posture without having to invest in their own security infrastructure as in on-prem users.  

Ten years ago, CIOs may have been hesitant to move to the cloud due to the uncertainty in stable safeguarding, but now we see a shift towards embracing the cloud for its enhanced security features. Cloud providers handle the distribution and implementation of patches, ensuring that the business can operate without interrupting the business. 

Moving to the cloud is not just a technical process, but a transformative one that keeps businesses current, up to date, and secure. 

 

#3 Myth: ERP is a one-size-fits-all solution. 

Most companies out there are struggling with a ‘spaghetti’ ERP landscape, hindering their ability to rapidly decode and transform to meet ever-changing business requirements.  Businesses see the transformational benefits of implementing a fit-to-standard approach to ERP, which helps maintain a clean core while leaving complexity behind.  However, it is important to note that ERP is not a one-size-fits-all solution, and deviations from standardization occur for a reason. 

Within the organization, it is essential to establish a clear understanding that cloud ERP doesn’t require businesses to adopt a complete fit-to-standard approach. 90% of fit standard approach and the rest focused 10% serve as a competitive edge or a key to enhancing operational efficiency. 

An example of a successful cloud ERP implementation is La Moderna, a food manufacturer in Mexico City. Their proactive adoption of best practices and simplification of processes has positioned them well to take advantage of future innovations in the ERP solution. The company’s attitude toward change management with a solid cloud ERP strategy enabled La Moderna to drive out complexity and positioned themselves to take advantage of the innovations in the solution. 

Cloud ERP enables businesses to stay compliant with the evolving rules and regulations, as localization extends beyond language or currency considerations but also encompasses compliance with local regulations. 

 

To learn more, please join us at the Secrets to Successful ERP Implementation webinar on May 22nd. 

This session will provide an overview of the capabilities SAP brings to help you implement ERP successfully with confidence. Shifting from a blueprint and design approach to a fit-to-standard approach comes with many efficiencies and advantages, and you’ll understand the key to implementing your ERP successfully from our expert. Furthermore, you'll learn how La Moderna successfully implemented our Cloud ERP smoothly and realized its value quickly.

entry.title: How to replicate your Fiori Inbox in Microsoft Teams
entry.content:
Using Looply you can replicate your Fiori Inbox in Teams, and it’s fast and easy.

Putting outstanding workitems in the face of those users who are not logged into SAP Work Zone / Fiori LaunchPad /  SAPGUI can accelerate processes and lead to a host of other benefits.

We can build the solution in 4 steps.

[1] Consider the trigger points.

You have options.  You might run a background job periodically to update Teams.  This is easy to set up, but resource-intensive if you decide to run it every few minutes instead of, say, twice per day.

You could trigger the Teams card from SAP workflow, so that you can be sure that the card is updated when a workitem is created and/or completed.  This would mean adding tasks to each workflow, so it’s considerably more work, but much less resource intensive, as there is no background job to run.

You could trigger the Teams card from SAP events – in exactly the same way that workflows are triggered, so that each time a critical workflow is triggered then the Teams card is created or updated.  This is less work to set up, but potentially more complex because you might be triggering the Teams card before the workflow has determined the user. (So you might want to build in a 60-second wait.)

Of course, you could choose more than one approach, as some processes are more critical than others.  For example, you might trigger a card update at the end of specific workflow processes, and also schedule a job twice a day.

In this example we’ll write a simple ABAP program to schedule as a background job. (See step 4.)

[2] Build your Looply Workflow.

Pure notification workflows tend to be very simple, as basically Looply is being passed some data, which it is mapping to Teams card fields, and push the card to the identified user.

In this example we’re going to add a button to enable the user to remove the card, and also the capability to either CREATE a new card where one doesn’t exist, or UPDATE an existing card.

This way there will never be more than one notification card for a user.

Let’s break this down.

  1. When the workflow is triggered (and no existing card is in place) then the first step is to trigger a new card.  
  2. The card could be closed manually (‘MS Teams Response’) or it could be subsequently updated from another SAP call (‘External Response')
  3. For the ‘External Response’ we need to capture the new data and push it into the data schema (‘payload’) that the card is mapped to.  We do this mapping in the ‘Override Payload’ step.
  4. Then we Update the card, using the new data.
  5. Again, the user could close the card manually, or more data might be received from SAP.  If more data is supplied then we update the schema from the data returned, and loop back to where we update the card.

 [3] Build your Adaptive Card in Looply

The card is really simple as there are very few variables.  It is basically a list of actions with URLS / deep links to point back to Fiori Inbox items.

This card comprises 5 containers, which are used to group and style fields:

  1. Heading
  2. User name
  3. Item count
  4. Item table
  5. Action buttons

The item table has just one field, for description, which is bound to “[${wi_text}](${wi_full})” such that the workitem text (wi_text) is displayed with the URL to the Workitem for action (wi_full).

[4] Build the SAP trigger

There are two parts to this. Firstly the Process Determination table should be configured, adding the Looply workflow ID.

Secondly, the trigger program:

Here we have created a simple selection screen just with the user name as the selection option.

The program logic is:

For each selected user:

  • Get details using BAPI_USER_GET_DETAILS
  • Get inbox items using SAP_WAPI_CREATE_WORKLIST
  • For each item, build the URL to the workitem in Fiori Inbox
  • Prepare data fields for the card in an internal table
  • Read the Looply Process Determination table
  • Trigger Looply workflow using /LOOPLY/CORE=>TRIGGER_WF for Outbound Scenario.  This tries to start a new workflow for the user, but fails if there is already a workflow running.
  • If that fails trigger Looply workflow using /LOOPLY/CORE=>TRIGGER_WF for Resume Scenario.

This will continue the Looply workflow from the current step.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report Z_LOOPLY_INBOX_DEMO
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Demo code only
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT z_looply_inbox_demo.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_card_items,
         workitem TYPE string,
         wi_full  TYPE string,
         wi_text  TYPE string,
       END OF ty_card_items.

TYPES: tty_card_items TYPE TABLE OF ty_card_items WITH NON-UNIQUE DEFAULT KEY.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_card_data,
         user  TYPE string,
         name  TYPE string,
         email TYPE string,
         count TYPE string,
         items TYPE tty_card_items,
       END OF ty_card_data.

DATA: ls_card_items     TYPE ty_card_items,
      lt_card_items     TYPE TABLE OF ty_card_items,
      ls_card_data      TYPE ty_card_data,
      lv_items          TYPE i,
      lt_usr01          TYPE TABLE OF usr01 WITH HEADER LINE,
      ls_usr01          TYPE usr01,
      lv_subrc          TYPE sysubrc,
      lt_worklist       TYPE TABLE OF swr_wihdr,
      ls_worklist       TYPE swr_wihdr,
      lv_data           TYPE string,
      ls_looply_trigger TYPE /looply/activity,
      lv_process_id     TYPE /looply/process_id,
      lv_recipient      TYPE ad_smtpadr,
      ls_history        TYPE /looply/history,
      ls_address        type BAPIADDR3,
      lt_return         type bapiret2_t.

SELECT-OPTIONS: s_uname FOR lt_usr01-bname.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  SELECT * FROM usr01 INTO TABLE lt_usr01 WHERE bname IN s_uname.

  LOOP AT lt_usr01 INTO ls_usr01.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_USER_GET_DETAIL'
  EXPORTING
    username                = ls_usr01-bname
  IMPORTING
    ADDRESS                 = ls_address
  tables
    return                  = lt_return.

    CLEAR: ls_card_items, lt_card_items[], ls_card_data.

    CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_WAPI_CREATE_WORKLIST'
      EXPORTING
        user        = ls_usr01-bname
        language    = sy-langu
      IMPORTING
        return_code = lv_subrc
      TABLES
        worklist    = lt_worklist.

    CHECK lv_subrc EQ 0.
    lv_items = lines( lt_worklist ).
    CHECK lv_items GT 0.

    READ TABLE lt_worklist INTO ls_worklist INDEX 1.

* Prepare card data - header level

    ls_card_data-user = ls_usr01-bname.
    ls_card_data-name = ls_address-fullname.
    ls_card_data-email = ls_address-e_mail.
    ls_card_data-count = lv_items.

* Item level

    LOOP AT lt_worklist INTO ls_worklist.
      ls_card_items-workitem = ls_worklist-wi_id.
      shift ls_card_items-workitem LEFT DELETING LEADING '0'.

      concatenate: '<link to inbox app>'
      ls_worklist-wi_id
      '/TaskCollection(SAP__Origin='
      `'LOCAL_TGW',InstanceID=` `'`
      ls_worklist-wi_id `'`
      ')'
      into ls_card_items-wi_full.

      ls_card_items-wi_text  = ls_worklist-wi_text.
      APPEND ls_card_items TO lt_card_items.
    ENDLOOP.

    ls_card_data-items = lt_card_items.

* Call Looply.

    lv_data = /ui2/cl_json=>serialize( data = ls_card_data pretty_name = /ui2/cl_json=>pretty_mode-low_case ).

    SELECT SINGLE * FROM /looply/activity INTO ls_looply_trigger
      WHERE scenario = 'OO'
      AND   scenario_id = 'WF_INBOX'
      AND   scenario_version = '00'
      AND   step = '1'.

    lv_recipient = ls_card_data-email.
    lv_process_id = ls_card_data-user.

      CALL METHOD /looply/core=>trigger_wf
        EXPORTING
          im_recipient         = lv_recipient
          im_looply_wf         = ls_looply_trigger-looply_wf
          im_looply_wf_version = ls_looply_trigger-looply_wf_version
          im_scenario          = ls_looply_trigger-scenario
          im_scenario_id       = ls_looply_trigger-scenario_id
          im_scenario_version  = ls_looply_trigger-scenario_version
          im_step              = ls_looply_trigger-step
          im_process_id        = lv_process_id
          im_data              = lv_data
        IMPORTING
          ex_subrc             = lv_subrc.

   IF lv_subrc EQ 0.
        WRITE: / 'Trigger: ',  lv_process_id.
   else.
      CALL METHOD /looply/core=>resume_wf
        EXPORTING
          im_process_id       = lv_process_id
          im_data             = lv_data
          im_scenario         = 'OO'
          im_scenario_id      = 'WF_INBOX'
          im_scenario_version = '00'
          im_step             = '1'
        IMPORTING
          ex_subrc            = lv_subrc.
      WRITE: / 'Resume: ',  lv_process_id.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

Summary

Now you are ready to test and roll out to specific groups of users.

Perhaps you could use a user parameter as an opt-in to receive Teams notifications – it’s probably not for everyone.

Since this is a one-way communication scenario, with only outbound data from SAP, the initial set up is really easy, with no authentication set up needed.

So you could be enjoying the benefits of Teams integration in days.

entry.title: Sapphire 2024: Make the Connections That Make the Most of Your Supply Chain
entry.content:
Bring Out Your Best.  A simple statement that holds a powerful truth to it, one that is the pivotal theme to this year’s SAP Sapphire and ASUG Conference.  When people think of their best, what comes to mind?  Perhaps personal success in the form of accolades and rewards; to others, reaching a destination at the end of their journey.  For us here in SAP Supply Chain Management, we want one thing: to break down silos!

Whether we realize it or not, supply chain impacts an entire company, and efficient processes cannot be run through siloed processes with expected success. However, good news – there’s something companies can do about it.

Find out how SAP breaks down those walls to turn supply chains into a strategic, sustainable asset and what you can expect to learn and experience at the Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference.

Leveraging Artificial Intelligence to Drive Business Innovation

Over the last year, Generative AI and AI have been the hottest topic in board rooms across the world, sparking conversations on the latest innovations and technologies that companies can utilize to promote efficiency and drive out complacency.   AI’s relevancy in supply chain is growing daily, addressing challenges from design to operate, as it helps augment the workforce in warehouses and factories through data-driven tools, empowering people within the supply chain to make more informed and timely decisions.

Technologies, as such, have the power to be a transformative force in organizations’ supply chains.  In its utilization, companies can develop smarter assets and products, driving out process inefficiencies, and connecting real-time information to their equipment and products, allowing for innovative business models.  Attendees can now see this technology in action at Sapphire 2024 at these enticing sessions:

SAP and Google Cloud Technology Accelerates AI-Driven Supply Chains

Navigating Today’s Logistics World with Data and AI

Supercharge Field Service Management Operations with AI

I’m Still Standing: Resilience Built into Your Supply Chain

Expect the unexpected has been a statement that’s rung true through supply chains globally. Disruptions can come in many forms – from geopolitical unrest to a surge in demand – both qualifying as a disturbance to your processes. Companies now need to have the ability to not only sense, identify, or predict these disruptions, but also have the visibility built in to adapt to them, as well. Understanding and pinpointing critical areas of risk and opportunity is key to an agile supply chain, as building resiliency into business processes allows companies to stand the test of time.

Companies now have the ability to see disruptions before they happen and have the systems in place to not only sense it but respond to it.  One of the main ways to drive resiliency throughout your supply chain is having those foundational interconnections between those systems, especially between the functional areas of your business.   Interoperability throughout your ecosystem is key, and Sapphire attendees have the chance to learn more at these exciting sessions:

Expand Your Business with a Risk-Resilient and AI-Enabled Supply Chain

Enhancing Supply Chain Resilience with AI and Freight Visibility

Achieve Supply Chain Resiliency Through Planning Excellence

Let’s Talk: Interoperability Across Your Systems

Scalability at large. Rapid deployment of innovation. Ease of adoption. One may question the connection between these three, and they all come to one word: Cloud. SAP’s Cloud ERP allows companies to work in an interoperable system, letting them work across end-to-end departments and solutions to manage cross-functional business processes. In a sense, when organizations successfully integrate the entire supply chain process – from design, planning, and manufacturing to logistics, maintenance, and service – and then integrate that into the rest of the business via native, in-depth ERP, they can drive differentiation.

In today’s digital age, why is this important?

It allows companies to run as an intelligent enterprise, which, in turns, connects the processes that promote better contextualized decisions.  These vital decisions are not only based on data from the business side, but in conjunction with the organizations’ operational side, as well.  This holistic approach promotes internal connectivity, opening the door for a seamless interconnected ecosystem, where organizations can transform the information without complex systems and multiple providers.   Those attending Sapphire can learn more about driving interoperability in a Cloud based environment at these innovative sessions:

Revolutionizing Supply Chains Using RISE with SAP at BBC

Unleash Your Potential with Enterprise-Wide Adoption of Cloud Solutions

Embarking on a Digital Manufacturing Transformation at King’s Hawaiian

The ability to keep up with the ever-evolving and growing rate of change – for instance, sustainability mandates and regulations – plus leveraging Business AI in the Cloud – in essence, having the embedded technology across all your systems and solutions to leverage real-time data and cutting-edge innovation to grow at scale – can not only change the way companies do business, but allow them to bring out their best.

Companies, organizations, executives, and attendees alike now have the chance to learn more about the cutting-edge technology that’s SAP is bringing to offer.  From AI to resiliency to interoperability, companies can better navigate the challenges they face and rapidly adopt to the evolving digital business environment.  At SAP Sapphire, you’ll see how you build a supply chain that will stand the test of time, shift to business-changing transformation, embed cutting-edge innovation across your departments, and, at the core, bring out your best.

We hope to see you in Orlando at SAP Sapphire & ASUG Annual Conference.  Click here to register.

entry.title: Best practices to follow during DTV implementation
entry.content:
Best practices to follow during DTV implementation

Data Transition Validation or DTV tool can expedite the data validation process by pointing out the differences between source and target system state, hence eases the reconciliation process. Drawing experience from many different customers, we came up with some of the best practices customers can follow for optimum result.

These are some of the best practices which can be followed for maximum benefits.

During the conversion or upgrade planning

  • Understand the scope of the DTV tool early in the project planning phase. Check the blog for more information regarding the tool Business data validation using DTV tool
  • Finalize the test scope, which means finalize the reports which you would like to validate using DTV tool early in the project planning.
  • Finalize the data scope(volumes) that you want to validate, for example how many company codes, GL accounts, Fiscal year etc. should be used during the DTV configuration.
  • Use DTV tool for data validation in each cycle of the project (such as dress rehearsal, performance run cycle etc) and improve the DTV run time (execution time) in each cycle to get optimum effect in the production run.
  • It is worth to mention that initial DTV test set up consume some time and this “estimated time” must be included in the project planning phase.

During DTV System preparation

  • It is recommended to use the latest and greatest version of the DTV tool. More info in SAP Note: 3117879 - Data Transition Validation DTV Tool - Central Note
  • Ensure to implement all DTV related SAP Notes for smooth execution.
  • Utilize the system resources such as executing server and executing group wisely to fine tune the performance of the DTV execution time.
  • Maintain optimal number of background jobs. The reports are executed via background jobs in DTV.
  • Make sure to maintain recommended values for SHMA configuration and memory parameter set up (RZ11)
    • rdisp/PG_KEEP - value = 30
    • rdisp/PG_MAXFS - value = 256000
    • rdisp/PG_SHM - value = 256000
    • abap/shared_objects_size_MB - value = 1800

You can also follow SAP Note 3226794 - SYSTEM_SHM_AREA_OBSOLETE error while using the DTV tool

  • Import the latest pre-delivered content.3258383 - DTV : SAP Pre- delivered Content
  • In the initial cycles, it would be good to have a copy of the source system post DTV source execution for reconciliation purposes later in the project (not mandatory, but recommended)
  • Make use of project export/ import functionality of DTV tool to save time and effort. Once a project is configured in one system, it can be exported and then imported to the subsequent system of the system landscape easily with this functionality.

During DTV configuration

  • For faster extraction and good performance split the data into small chunks to avoid long running jobs. e.g., Create multiple entry for company code in global project data.
  • Import the report multiple times in case of different variant configured for same report.
  • If you are using variant for report execution, make sure it exists in all the systems where DTV runs. 
  • DTV works only for the ALV report outputs. Make sure the variant you choose for report output is not classical list but one of the ALV outputs. For example: RFBILA00, by default has List output. If you use this in variant, then DTV will not result any data.
  • You can create multiple project global data definition for the same parameter. For example, company_code for fixed assets, company code for MM, and have different set of relevant company codes.
  • DTV executes a given report for each combination of values for the split parameter maintained. Hence you can control the granularity/ data volume of the execution by providing appropriate splits.
  • If the same parameter is specified in Split , variant and conditions : DTV will evaluate as mentioned in the help documentation
  • Use Simulation, until you find the right split size. It is not needed to execute Simulation post this.
  • Ensure to define same input parameter for source and target system.
  • Ensure not to change the project global data in target system.
  • Make use of exclude parameter to specify fields that would be excluded from the validation.
  • Custom report with ALV output can also be validated using DTV tool starting DTV 1.4             Business Data Validation of Custom Reports using DTV
  • For custom reports, adjust the output keys in case the evaluation is not happening with the right keys.

During DTV execution

  •  If Simulation is executed for any report, you can use the Import Simulation Results to avoid a   second time extraction. Check the blog for more information on simulation                         Simulation made easy in DTV 1.4
  • The result data shows only 10 K records on load. In case you want to see all the records, click on the refresh button in the ALV toolbar.

Post DTV Execution

  • “DTV is not a reconciliation tool, but is a validation tool”, hence, in case of differences pointed out by the tool, user may consider following below steps as a quick check.
    • Run the report in SE38 with same input parameters.
    • Then, compare the results (obtained from SE38) with the DTV tool results.
    • Reconcile the results in both source (copy of a source system) and target system to ensure the correctness of the tool.

Conclusion

DTV is an easy-to-use yet powerful tool which can help in business data reconciliation by showing the differences between the source and the target system automatically. After a successful Evaluation, DTV can only point out the discrepancies in the Target system (if any) and it is up to the users to perform the reconciliation.

entry.title: SAP Mentor Interview with Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Earlier this month I had the opportunity to interview Eric van Rossum, SAP’s Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP. You can watch the video of our discussion and read the highlights below, where you’ll also find related learning materials. Enjoy!

Eric van Rossum is the Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP at SAP, overseeing product marketing for the company's cloud ERP portfolio. He serves as the strategic bridge between product development and customer acquisition, ensuring product messaging and positioning are attuned to customer needs, and facilitating the successful launch and adoption of products in the market. Eric has been with SAP for 24 years and brings a wealth of experience across various functions including consulting, sales, corporate strategy, and support.

To help me summarize the content of the interview, I've used SAP Generative AI Hub :-). Check here what it ays are the highlights of our conversation: 

  • SAP has two cloud ERP offerings: RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP
  • RISE with SAP is targeted toward SAP’s installed-base customers, providing them a path to cloud ERP at their pace.
  • GROW with SAP is targeted toward net new customers to SAP, and installed base customers who want to take a greenfield approach to cloud ERP.
  • SAP is focusing on making ERP an enabler for business model changes, driving sustainability, and improving efficiency, as these are the key customer needs currently in the market.
  • With RISE and GROW, customers should be able to update their software regularly, making them innovation-ready and efficient, especially with the increasing prevalence of AI.
  • Eric believes the shift to the cloud also requires a paradigm shift within companies, citing an example of a customer who moved towards a standardized system and adopted SAP's out-of-the-box solutions.
  • One of the significant hurdles for customers adopting cloud ERP is the organizational readiness to standardize their processes, which requires a significant cultural shift.
  • Moving ERP to the cloud is seen as risky by some companies due to the critical role ERP systems play in company operations. They need strategies to transition without disrupting their business.
  • Eric is interested in a continued dialogue with the SAP Community, to hear from end users and improve SAP’s offerings to meet their needs better.

If you’re going to Sapphire in Orlando or Barcelona, you can hear more from Eric in these sessions:

SAP Sapphire Orlando (June 3-5)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP169)

  • Supercharge Your Business and Get Innovation Ready with RISE with SAP (Session code: ERP168)

  • The New ERP Mindset (Session code: TRE321)

SAP Sapphire Barcelona (June 11-13)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP146)

If you’re planning to join Sapphire virtual, you can hear more in these sessions:

  • ERP126 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Discover planned innovations

  • ERP127 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Find out what is next

  • ERP200v | Accelerate business growth with S/4AHANA Cloud Public Edition

And to learn more about the topics discussed in this interview, the following learning journeys are especially recommended:

  • Exploring SAP Cloud ERP learning journey as a starting point to start learning about SAP Cloud ERP, how to distinguish SAP’s cloud ERP solutions and transformation offerings (GROW & RISE), establish a cloud mindset (fit-to-standard, SAP best practices, integration and extensibility, clean core), and get insights into business AI and sustainability fundamentals
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition learning journey teaching fundamental implementation knowledge for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition leading to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation consultant certification complemented by quarterly live sessions, stay certified content and assessments focusing on AI capabilities and intelligent ERP innovations
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey teaching implementation fundamentals for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition leading to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation consultant certification, complemented by stay certified content and assessments focusing on how to Manage a Clean Core

Those fundamentals learning journeys are complemented by various line of business specific learning offerings and certifications focusing on business processes, best practices content, and configuration/integration/extensibility options

entry.title: VAT registration number in Billing (VBRK-STCEG) - inconsistent explanation of standard logic
entry.content:
The VAT Registration number in the billing document (technical filed VBRK-STCEG) is automatically determined by SAP standard functionality when the billing document is created. This functionality can be configured according to business requirements through customization settings available at:

SPRO -> Sales and Distribution -> Basic Functions -> Taxes -> Maintain Determination of Value-Added Tax registration number

Various rules can be applied for configuring VAT ID determination. These rules allow the VAT ID to be sourced from the Payer, Sold-to party, or Ship-to party data, or so called "priority rule" can be applied. I will not focus on the description of each rule as it's not the purpose of this blog. However, a comprehensive description is available in the performance assistant in SPRO.

In the considered scenario, a standard priority rule is used which works in the following way (according to the system description):

1. If the payer has a VAT ID number and RG <> AG, then the tax number and tax classification are copied from the payer (in this case, the ship-to party is not relevant). The tax number is taken according to the 'tax country of destination'.

2. If 1. does not apply: If the ship-to party has a VAT registration number and the sold-to party does not, tax number and tax classification are copied from the ship-to party.

3. If 2. does not apply: Tax number and tax classification are copied from the sold-to party.

We want to analyze a case where the VAT numbers of the payer and sold-to party are identical, while the ship-to party has a different VAT registration number. According to the outlined rules, it is expected that VBRK-STCEG will be populated with the VAT ID of the sold-to party. This is explained as follows:

  • Point 1 does not apply because the VAT ID of the sold-to party and the payer are identical.
  • Point 2 does not apply because the sold-to party has a VAT ID (the rule suggests that both conditions must be met : "If the ship-to party has a VAT registration number and the sold-to party does not"). 
  • Point 3 is applicable as the sold-to part has VAT ID. 

The description of point 2 suggests, that it does not apply because the sold-to party has a VAT registration number. However, it turns out that standard logic does not work as described. Let's investigate it based on an example in the system.

Note: The data in the this example is fictional. 

As mentioned earlier, sold-to and ship-to party has different VAT IDs in master data:

Yet, when the billing document is created, the VAT ID is read from the ship-to party rather than the sold-to party, contrary to the rules description:

Let's take a closer look at what is behind it. The program logic which fills data in VBRK-STCEG is located in include LV60AA95, form vbrk_vbrp_datentransport:

When we look at the program and find the part of the code responsible for the execution of rule 2, we notice that the logical operator 'OR' is used instead of 'AND'. Consequently, the fact that the sold-to party has a valid VAT ID does not affect the determination logic, and in this case, the VAT ID is taken from the ship-to party's data:

 
Considering the above, this discrepancy may lead to misunderstandings of SAP's standard logic and can sometimes confuse business users and even consultants. In my professional experience, I have already encountered this issue several times, and usually it was reported by users as a system bug. Hopefully, this post will help others gain a better understanding of VAT ID determination in SAP ECC.
 

Note: This article does not apply to SAP S/4HANA, where the VAT ID determination logic has been restructured, and the explanation of the rules has been rewritten.

entry.title: How to get Tables used inside a t-code?
entry.content:
After an extensive search for a suitable program to effectively display program tables, I decided to take matters into my own hands. Through careful planning and execution, I've developed a small custom solution that streamlines the process of identifying tables necessary for generating tailored reports to meet the unique needs of each customer. I welcome any further suggestions or feedback on this endeavor.

Code listing for: ZCUST_TCODE_TO_TABLES
Description: Program to get TABLES used in a TCODE

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZCUST_TCODE_TO_TABLES
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Owner        : Surendra Karma
*& Note         : This is just a test program. I'm writing as per my
*&                understanding. This can not guarantee that all the
*&                tables are captured. Suggestions are always Appreciated!
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zcust_tcode_to_tables.

DATA: lv_tcode TYPE tstc-tcode.
SELECT-OPTIONS: s_tcode FOR lv_tcode NO INTERVALS OBLIGATORY.
PARAMETERS : c_transp AS CHECKBOX,
             c_inttab AS CHECKBOX,
             c_view   AS CHECKBOX,
             c_append AS CHECKBOX,
             c_all    AS CHECKBOX DEFAULT abap_true.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
  IF s_tcode IS NOT INITIAL.
    SELECT COUNT( * ) FROM tstc UP TO 1 ROWS WHERE tcode IN @s_tcode.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE 'Please enter a valid t-code' TYPE 'I' DISPLAY LIKE 'E'.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  SELECT tstc~tcode,
         tstct~ttext,
         tstc~pgmna,
         d010tab~tabname,
         dd02l~tabclass,
         dd02l~sqltab,
         dd02t~ddtext
     FROM tstc AS tstc
     INNER JOIN tstct AS tstct ON tstct~tcode = tstc~tcode
     INNER JOIN d010tab AS d010tab ON d010tab~master = tstc~pgmna
     INNER JOIN dd02l AS dd02l ON dd02l~tabname = d010tab~tabname
     INNER JOIN dd02t AS dd02t ON dd02t~tabname = d010tab~tabname
     INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_data)
     WHERE tstc~tcode IN @s_tcode
       AND dd02t~ddlanguage = 'E'
  AND tstct~sprsl = 'E'.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    SORT lt_data BY tcode tabclass tabname DESCENDING.
    IF c_all IS INITIAL.
      IF c_transp IS INITIAL.
        DELETE lt_data WHERE tabclass = 'TRANSP'.
      ENDIF.
      IF c_inttab IS INITIAL.
        DELETE lt_data WHERE tabclass = 'INTTAB'.
      ENDIF.
      IF c_view IS INITIAL.
        DELETE lt_data WHERE tabclass = 'VIEW'.
      ENDIF.
      IF c_append IS INITIAL.
        DELETE lt_data WHERE tabclass = 'APPEND'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

  DATA lo_salv TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table.

  TRY.
      cl_salv_table=>factory(
        IMPORTING
          r_salv_table   = lo_salv
        CHANGING
          t_table        = lt_data
      ).
    CATCH cx_salv_msg INTO DATA(lo_excp). " ALV: General Error Class with Message
      MESSAGE lo_excp TYPE 'E' DISPLAY-LIKE 'I'.
  ENDTRY.

  lo_salv->get_functions( )->set_all( abap_true ).
  lo_salv->get_display_settings( )->set_striped_pattern( abap_true ).
  lo_salv->get_columns( )->set_optimize( abap_true ).

  lo_salv->get_selections( )->set_selection_mode( if_salv_c_selection_mode=>row_column ).

  lo_salv->display( ).


*Selection texts
*----------------------------------------------------------
* C_ALL         All tables/structure
* C_APPEND      Append structures
* C_INTTAB      Internal tables/Structures
* C_TRANSP      Transparent tables
* C_VIEW        Views
* S_TCODE       Transaction Codes


*Messages
*----------------------------------------------------------
* Message class: Hard coded

Extracted by Direct Download Enterprise version 1.3.1 - E.G.Mellodew. 1998-2005 UK. Sap Release 757

entry.title: Discover the World of Poultry: From Farm to Fork with E2E Supply chain in SAP S4HANA Private Cloud
entry.content:
Implementing SAP S4HANA by adopting baseline best practices to build solution with minor customizations in the poultry industry involves managing the entire supply chain and logistic from purchasing of raw materials, vaccines and medicines etc. production of different feeds based on requirement of broilers and breeders, procuring or producing eggs from breeders, producing chicks from egg, growing chicks to chicken and slaughtering of chicken to produce poultry products to consumers. Using SAP S4HANA, this can be effectively managed through various integrated modules namely MM, FI, CO, PP, QM, SD, PS and SAC. Here’s a comprehensive approach to achieving a proper solution in poultry using SAP S4HANA capabilities. The SAP baseline best practices approach ensures processes required to deploy by scope items and can be implemented which cover the entire poultry supply chain with financial. Below holistic view in Verticals of Poultry industry will give us crisp understanding.

Verticals of Poultry are:

Feed Mill:

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure and maintaining inventory of corn, soya, vitamins, minerals, produced finished feeds. SAP production planning (PP) for formulations as recipes, consume raw materials and produce feeds with batch. SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for Feed. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell Feeds to out growers, customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Rearing and Breeding (Parent birds):

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure parent birds and maintaining inventory of feeds, medicines, vitamins, minerals, disinfectants, produced hatchable eggs, SAP production planning (PP) for rearing birds & laying birds with recording of culls and mortality. consume feed, medicines, vitamins, minerals and producing hatchable eggs with batch, SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for rearing and growing. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell hatchable eggs and manure to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Hatchery:

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure hatchable eggs and maintaining inventory of vaccines, medicines, vitamins, minerals, disinfectants, produced chicks (DOC), SAP production planning (PP) for sorting, setting, hatching with recording of culls and mortality. consume vaccines, medicines and producing Chicks (DOC) with batch, SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for Hatching. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell DOC and Unhatched eggs to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Broiler:

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure chicks (DOC) and maintaining inventory of vaccines, medicines, vitamins, minerals, disinfectants, produced broilers (live bird), SAP production planning (PP) for growing birds with recording of culls and mortality. consume vaccines, medicines and producing broilers (live bird) with batch, SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for broilers. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell broilers (live bird) and manure to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Processing and Packaging Plant (PPL):

with SAP Material Management (MM) to procure broilers (live bird) and maintaining inventory of spices, packing, disinfectants, produced products, SAP production planning (PP) for slaughtering, grading, cutting, deboning etc and consume spices, packing and producing finished products with batch, SAP Quality Management module (QM) to record inspection results for procured and in process inspections for poultry products. SAP Sales and distribution (SD) to sell poultry products to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability. Manage stock transfers from Processing plant to DCs with SAP Material Management (MM) and SAP Sales and distribution (SD).

Distribution centers to Sales outlets or retail outlets:

Van sales, wholesale, Retail customer outlets with SAP Sales and Distribution (SD) to Manage sales orders and distribution sell poultry products to customers and Finance (FI) and Controlling (CO) for cost and profitability.

Implementation Steps

Assessment and Planning: Conduct a thorough assessment of current organisation structure and define the scope items to be used.

System Design: Design the SAP solution architecture with baseline best practices and customize to the specific needs of the poultry supply chain.

Customization and Configuration: Customize SAP modules to fit the unique requirements of the poultry industry with catch weight management, PPDS, aATP, Segmentation etc.

Integration: Integrate SAP with existing systems, IoT devices, and third-party applications.

Data Migration: Migrate existing data into the SAP system, ensuring data integrity and completeness with LTMC.

Training and Change Management: Provide training to users and manage the transition to the new system with enable now.

Go-Live and Continuous Improvement: Launch the system and continuously monitor and improve processes based on feedback and data insights.

Benefits

Revenue on investment: Investing on SAP S4HANA private cloud will turn your investment cost to Operating Cost where no need to invest on hardware and baseline best practices provides more functionalities with less maintenance cost rather Investing in multiple software systems

Single source of truth: Streamlined operations with respect to processes can be run in S4HANA without multiple systems (third party).

Better Decision-Making: Data-driven insights can be made available from single source for strategic planning and operational improvements.

Mobile Applications: Deploy SAP Fiori apps for mobile, tablet, PC to access critical data and operations to manage on-the-go.

Conclusion

Using SAP S4HANA private Cloud in the poultry industry ensures a comprehensive, integrated approach to managing the entire supply chain. By leveraging SAP’s robust ERP capabilities and advanced technologies the poultry businesses can achieve greater efficiency, traceability, and profitability. Successful implementation requires careful planning to utilize baseline best practices with customization and continuous improvement to adapt to changing market demands and regulatory requirements.

entry.title: No todo en SAP es ABAP
entry.content:
Buen día tengan todos ustedes, a manera de introducción es esta entrada donde, en mi  experiencia en el mundo de SAP me he topado con diferentes solicitudes dirigidas hacia los equipos de desarrollo ABAP, las cuales no requieren estrictamente del desarrollo sino un poco mas de calma  y persistencia en encontrar donde es que el sistema estándar permite la configuración, así como definir la manera mas adecuada de solventar la incidencia, a manera de mención de algunas enumero dos ( solo como mención mas adelante se profundizara en el tema )

Ocultar campo en transacción VA01:
En esta ocacion se nos solicito la búsqueda de alguna EXIT la cual sirviera para realizar esta tarea.    respuesta rápida Tx. SHD0

Sustitución de datos en documentos financieros:
Si bien dentro del estándar de SAP en la Tx. OBBH existe la posibilidad de colocar código en las EXITS de SUSTITUCIÓN (el famoso programa RGGBS000) también dentro de la misma en cada paso definido se tiene la posibilidad de realizar una validación bastante completa de cuando aplicara la misma.


Pues bien siendo esta la primera entrada quisiera aclarar que cualquier tipo de comentario a favor o en contra de lo que se publique sera bienvenido ya que aquí serán publicados casos con mucha influencia de la forma de pensar del autor osea yo quien solo soy una persona mas dentro del obscuro mundo de SAP, y si estamos aquí es para aprender mas y colaborar mutuamente para un mejor domino de las herramientas.


Saludos y Éxito en sus casos.
El Panda
